pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property to target JDK 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: The project is migrating to Java 21; without this change, the compiler and runtime will target Java 17, failing to utilize or comply with Java 21 requirements.

src/test/java/com/training/ecommerce/ser.general_idea: Migrate JUnit 4 imports to JUnit 5 (Jupiter) equivalents.
src/test/java/com/training/ecommerce/ser.why_exists: JUnit 4 classes (org.junit.*) are not part of the JUnit 5 API; using them with JUnit 5 runners or in a project configured for JUnit 5 will result in compilation errors or test discovery failures.
The @Before annotation is from JUnit 4 and is ignored by the JUnit 5 engine, causing setup methods to not run.

src/test/java/com/training/ecommerce/ser.general_idea: Replace static Assert calls with JUnit 5 Assertions calls.
src/test/java/com/training/ecommerce/ser.why_exists: org.junit.Assert is JUnit 4; if the import is removed or the class is not on the classpath, these calls will fail to compile.

src/test/java/com/training/ecommerce/ser.general_idea: Replace JUnit 4 expected exception style with JUnit 5 assertThrows.
src/test/java/com/training/ecommerce/ser.why_exists: The 'expected' attribute on @Test is a JUnit 4 feature and is not supported in JUnit 5, leading to tests that do not verify exceptions correctly or compile errors if mixed improperly.

src/test/java/com/training/ecommerce/ser.general_idea: Replace JUnit 4 expected exception style with JUnit 5 assertThrows for ResourceNotFoundException.
src/test/java/com/training/ecommerce/ser.why_exists: The 'expected' attribute on @Test is a JUnit 4 feature and is not supported in JUnit 5.
